### Post by m.h.shams on 2006-09-04
dear friends,

when i trying to dial with gnomeppp i got this error : NO CARRIER

anyone know what this mean ? and how can i fix it ?

i have a softmodem and installe slmodem app to run it.

my gnome-ppp log is heare : 
```

--> Ignoring malformed input line: ";Do NOT edit this file by hand!"
--> WvDial: Internet dialer version 1.55
--> Cannot get information for serial port.
--> Initializing modem.
--> Sending: ATZ
ATZ
OK
--> Sending: ATQ0 V1 E1 S0=0 &C1 &D2 +FCLASS=0
ATQ0 V1 E1 S0=0 &C1 &D2 +FCLASS=0
OK
--> Modem initialized.
--> Sending: ATM1L3DT9716120
--> Waiting for carrier.
ATM1L3DT9716120
NO CARRIER
--> No Carrier!  Trying again.
--> Maximum Attempts Exceeded..Aborting!!
--> Disconnecting at Mon Sep  4 21:39:14 2006


```

---

### Post by Lilac on 2006-09-04
Have you checked that you have configured wvdial properly? 

To check that you have configured properly type in terminal

```
sudo wvdialconf /etc/wvdial.conf
```

If the modem is found, type:

```
sudo nano /etc/wvdial.conf
```

Try adding Stupid mode = on. It helps with pppd.  You may need to add Carrier Check = no.

Give that a shot and see if it works.  I had the same problem also.

---

Modem Type = Analog Modem
Baud = 460800
New PPPD = yes
Modem = /dev/ttySL0
ISDN = 0

; Password = <Your Password>

anyone help me plz !!!

### Post by Lilac on 2006-09-07
ok... what you need to do is to get rid of ; at the start of the last three lines.  What that means is that you've commented those lines out and you dont want to do that.  Then fill in the details.  Put your dial out number, your internet login in name and your password in the places where it requires you to.

Give that a shot.  Do you have the internet connected on windows??  If so... use that password and login.

Good luck.
